classification.spec_type_rationale: pre-seeded from research handoff at research/2026-06-27-remove-leftover-electron-vite/handoff.json (user accepted)

spec_seeds.overview: Remove the leftover electron-vite scaffold debug handler from the Electron main process. The bug (bugs/002-leftover-ping-debug-artifact.md) is a dead no-op IPC handler — ipcMain.on('ping', () => console.log('pong')) — that violates the project's no-debug-artifacts rule. This spec deletes the handler, its // IPC test comment, and the now-unused ipcMain import.

acceptance_criteria.statement: The mintenvoy main process shall not register a 'ping' IPC handler.
WHEN the application starts, the mintenvoy main process shall create the BrowserWindow and complete its ready-event lifecycle exactly as before the change.
WHEN the main process initializes, the mintenvoy main process shall register no 'ping' IPC listener and emit no 'pong' log message.
The main process entry file shall contain no console-log debug artifact.
The mintenvoy codebase shall pass lint and type-check with no unused import remaining in the main process entry file.
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: npm run lint && npm run typecheck

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI/pipeline changes — source-only dead-code removal in src/main/index.ts
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No hooks or gates added or changed by this removal
ac_subsection_na.documentation: docs/main/index.md does not document the ping handler; no docs/ update is required after removal

constraints.content: App boot and window-creation flow (createWindow, the app ready event, browser-window-created, activate, and window-all-closed handlers) must not regress
Keep the change minimal: delete only the dead handler, its // IPC test comment, and the now-unused ipcMain import — do not modify unrelated main-process code

affected_areas.impact: Delete the ipcMain.on('ping', ...) handler (line 54) + its // IPC test comment (line 53); remove ipcMain from the line 1 electron import

out_of_scope.content: Replacing the dead handler with a real application IPC handler (research approach 2 — no current IPC need)
Adding test infrastructure for the main process to cover this removal (none exists today)
Any docs/ changes — the ping handler is undocumented, so no documentation update is needed
Manually updating the bugs/002-leftover-ping-debug-artifact.md record lifecycle (Open to Fixed is a manual step outside this spec)
Touching renderer or preload code, or any other ipcMain handler (none exist; ping is the sole undocumented dead scaffold)

risks.risk: Deleting the handler accidentally removes a still-needed import or alters the whenReady block, breaking app boot
risks.mitigation: Remove only ipcMain from the line 1 import (other imports stay); typecheck + lint + build gate the change; smoke-launch confirms the window still opens
